title: Phenomenon 7
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Item Materialization}}
{{Phenomenon Class||frequency=3|effectiveness=?|danger=1}}
[[File:Phenomenon 7 firearm.jpg|center|600x600px|link=]]

''Firearms are rare and valuable commodities generated by Phenomenon 7.''
== Description == {{Blurimage|file=Phenomenon 7 crate.jpg|caption=A crate of wood of seemingly no purpose.}} {{Blurimage|file=Phenomenon 7 crate 2.jpg|caption=An instance of Phenomenon 7 discovered on Level 7.}} Phenomenon 7, coined as the term "Item Materialization" describes the spontaneous occurrence of seemingly randomized objects appearing throughout the Backrooms. While it is still unknown whether or not this phenomena is a materialization of some sort or an unique no-clip of items belonging to that of baseline reality, this phenomenon is one of the few phenomena that has truly benefited humans in the backrooms. Phenomenon 7 is also the singular most common source of firearms and ammunition for many factions and lone wanderers alike (the scavenging of [[Entity 25]] is another common cited source). Crates of food or medical supplies are also highly valued among wanderers with factions being known to send dispatch teams for the sole purpose of finding useful drops and stock supplies. While the phenomenon is quite common to come across in comparison to many of the phenomena recorded across the database, it is still uncommon in the grand scheme of things; especially considering that the objects spawned in is completely randomized, rendering truly useful items being only a fraction of probability within encounters. While theoretically Phenomenon 7 should exist across all Backroom levels, due to the lack sample size, it is unknown if that is truly the case; despite this, the phenomenon does seemingly appear in numerous levels with seemingly no logic or quantifiable categorization. With limited data, some scholars have also proposed that the phenomenon usually appears on safer levels, or that the frequency/probability of Phenomenon 7's manifestation is tied to the survival difficulty, although skeptics point that the data of dangerous levels is simply skewed because of the frequent deaths of explorers, which significantly lowers the chance of recorded encounters. While commonly harmless and beneficial, there have been instances where Phenomenon 7 puts people in danger. Below is a comprehensive list of harmful cases recorded by the database. * Two wanderers were sprayed of acid after opening a pressurized can of soda on [[Level 4]], both were found and hospitalized by the S.R.C., resulting in 1 death. * An [[Aurielle]] member was killed after pocketing an grenade defect that exploded a few minutes after due to its unstable nature, what was left of the corpse was later discovered by the [[M.E.G.]] on [[Level 117]]. * A [[Backroom Colonists|Backroom Colonist]] was fell severely ill after opening a crate bursting of anomalous unsanitary content on [[Level 300]], she was subsequently hospitalized by the [[Backrooms Red Cross]] and is currently recovering. * A boat found on [[Level 7]] started to collapse with seemingly no apparent reason, resulting in the death of one [[Entity Research Committee]] member. * Several crates that contained hostile entities such as Clumps and Hounds were discovered on [[Level 20]] by the [[The Anti-Entity Group|A.E.G.]], although any similar instance was never recorded again. == Theories == === Stray Conveyance === This theory suggests that many, if not most missing items that were lost have been no-clipped to the Backrooms, just like how many humans have done so. The primary argument against this proposal is the existence of seemingly anomalous defects and abstract variations occasionally found in some items, usually variations that appears to be rather abstract and unconventional. For instance, a revolver may lack elements that a conventional revolvers would have, or not match with any known manufactured revolver type, functionally operating with 7 hatches; this made many researchers dismiss this theory as such items are non-existent in reality. Despite this, few still argue that these items have undergone changes due to the result of an unknown no-clip effect. === Corrupted Mimicry === This theory suggests that the Backrooms is naturally attempting to mimic objects used by humans in baseline reality, just like how it is attempting to mimic humans via [[Entity 9]], suggesting that [[Entity 9|Facelings]] are imperfect manifestations rather than alien entities, explaining the biological similarities between the two species; this logic can be applied to many other entities too, such as [[Entity 4]] and [[Entity 8]]. Many scholars have also pointed out that such concepts can be taken to a larger scale, as other similar theories including the Kauer Thesis pointing to similar conclusions: the Backrooms itself is a shifting purgatory mimicking and creating a twisted, corrupted copy of baseline reality. The general consensus is that this hypothesis is much more in line and consistent with the evidence of defects found in some items, with similar theories pointing to the same logic. === Unified Proposal === Recent discussions have brought up a new school of thought that joins both theories together. Supporters of this theory suggests that while some objects are indeed randomly no-clipped to the Backrooms, other items are mimics of the very objects no-clipped from reality, creating a truly diverse range of objects. Some researchers also point out that this theory may point to the creation completely new alien objects generated from already corrupted recreations, on the condition that the Backrooms does also mimic items native to its dimension, although no recorded instance of such as been discovered to this day.
